page: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 192 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 12 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA L4 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, featuring 24 GB of dedicated VRAM. This instance does not include local storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression tasks placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core operations are also robust, with stress-ng multi-core performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25%. Large block read memory bandwidth is strong at 223.85 GB/sec, while write bandwidth and database operations fall into the average tier. For AI workloads, the hardware achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 339.34 tokens/second on small language models.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of a modern NVIDIA L4 GPU and 192 GB of RAM offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy compute tasks that do not require local NVMe storage. The shared CPU allocation model balances compute availability with resource cost. This server is highly suited for machine learning inference, data science pipelines, video processing, and memory-intensive analytical workloads.
description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL4 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server designed for memory-intensive compute and machine learning workloads. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it features 16 vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 192 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM. The server does not include local storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. The high memory-to-core ratio of 12 GB per core makes this instance highly cost-efficient for data-heavy processing tasks. It is best suited for machine learning inference, data science, and memory-intensive analytical workloads.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB of VRAM, optimized for machine learning and data science.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-1xL4 is a KVM-based server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L4 GPU, built for machine learning.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual server combining high-capacity system memory with Ada Lovelace architecture for efficient machine learning and data analysis.
bullet_points: NVIDIA L4 GPU with 24 GB VRAM for accelerated workloads
16 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with top-tier single-core performance
192 GB of system memory providing 12 GB per core
No local storage included in the base configuration
Optimized for machine learning inference and data science pipelines
